top_stories.title: OAuth Client ID Spoofing Lets Attackers Validate Stolen Microsoft Entra Credentials
top_stories.summary: A novel evasion technique called OAuth client ID spoofing is being actively exploited by at least two threat actors to validate stolen credentials in Microsoft Entra ID environments. The technique allows attackers to enumerate user accounts and authenticate with stolen credentials without triggering successful sign-in events, bypassing standard security telemetry and defender alerts.

top_stories.title: Microsoft is forcing an enterprise transition to passkeys
top_stories.summary: Microsoft Entra ID will replace SMS and voice authentication with passkeys starting in September, marking a forced enterprise transition away from traditional credential methods. The shift is driven by the increasing risk of AI-powered attacks against legacy authentication factors.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Entra ID gets passkeys default authentication starting September
top_stories.summary: Microsoft has announced that passkeys will become the default authentication method for Entra ID starting September 2026. This represents a major shift in enterprise authentication policy for one of the most widely deployed identity platforms, moving away from traditional password-based methods by default.

top_stories.title: Millions of Microsoft Entra Accounts Targeted in OAuth Client ID Spoofing Campaigns
top_stories.summary: Proofpoint has detailed an attack campaign targeting millions of Microsoft Entra accounts by spoofing OAuth client IDs to probe accounts and test credentials at cloud scale. The attacks are designed to bypass common sign-in detections, exploiting the OAuth authentication flow within Microsoft Entra environments.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Entra ID authentication overhaul to start in September 2026
top_stories.summary: Microsoft will begin rolling out passkeys as the default authentication experience for Microsoft Entra ID in the public cloud starting September 1, 2026. Organizations with SMS or voice authentication enabled will be automatically enabled for passkeys, changing the default authentication flow for affected tenants.

news.title: The script, not the voice, is what makes AI voice phishing work
news.summary: Researchers from Harvard Kennedy School, Meta, and others conducted a study with 4,100 US adults testing AI voice phishing scenarios using six commercial voice systems and human callers. The research found that the persuasive script content, not the AI-generated voice quality, is the primary factor that makes vishing attacks succeed, with scenarios involving social engineering tactics like urgent password reset requests proving effective.

news.title: AI Agents Broke the Security Playbook. Here's What Replaces It.
news.summary: Token Security argues that AI agents require a new approach to identity security, moving away from traditional security workflows toward a live identity foundation. The piece focuses on how AI agent environments demand dynamic, real-time identity management frameworks that security teams can customize to their own contexts.

news.title: Two Scattered Spider Hackers Get 5.5 Years Each for £29 Million TfL Hack
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider hacking group were sentenced to 5.5 years each for the 2024 hack of Transport for London, which compromised 148 systems and forced all 27,000 employees to reset passwords in person. The attack highlights the real-world operational impact of credential-based attacks and large-scale identity compromise requiring manual authentication recovery.

news.title: n8n Token Exchange Flaw Could Let Attackers Log In as Users From Another Issuer
news.summary: A security flaw in n8n's workflow automation platform allowed attackers to authenticate as arbitrary users by exploiting improper JWT validation on Enterprise instances configured with multiple external token issuers. The platform matched incoming JWTs solely on the `sub` claim while ignoring the `iss` (issuer) claim, meaning a valid token from one issuer could grant access to an account registered under a different issuer.

news.title: Zoom Patches Critical Windows Flaw That Could Enable Account Takeover
news.summary: Zoom patched a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-53412, CVSS 9.8) in Zoom Workplace for Windows that could enable account takeover. The flaw, caused by improper input validation, affects the Zoom Desktop Client for Windows, Zoom VDI Client for Windows, and Zoom Meeting SDK for Windows.

news.title: Identity Attacks Overtake Exploits as Top Ransomware Cause
news.summary: Identity-based attacks have surpassed exploits as the leading root cause of ransomware incidents. MFA was present in 97% of credential-based attacks yet failed to prevent compromise, highlighting critical gaps in authentication defenses.

news.title: Zoom warns of critical account takeover vulnerability
news.summary: Zoom disclosed a critical vulnerability in its Windows desktop client and SDK that allows unauthenticated attackers to perform account takeover. The flaw affects authentication mechanisms and enables session hijacking without requiring prior credentials.

news.title: Two SonicWall SMA 1000 Zero-Days Exploited, One Could Enable Admin Commands
news.summary: SonicWall has disclosed two actively exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in its Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 1000 series appliances, one rated CVSS 10.0. CVE-2026-15409 is a server-side request forgery flaw exploitable by remote unauthenticated attackers, with the second vulnerability potentially enabling arbitrary admin command execution.

news.title: Microsoft Patches Record 622 Flaws, Including Two Zero-Days Under Active Attack
news.summary: Microsoft released its largest-ever Patch Tuesday update, covering 622 CVEs including two zero-days already under active exploitation. The release is more than triple the previous record of around 200 CVEs from a single Patch Tuesday.

news.title: NIMC, IT firms push for secure digital government services
news.summary: Nigeria's National Identity Management Commission (NIMC) and IT firms are advocating for secure digital government services with a focus on digital identity and cybersecurity. The initiative emphasizes robust digital identity infrastructure as a component of improved public service delivery in Nigeria.

news.title: Delinea Launches New Partner Program to Drive Partner Profitability and Scale Growth
news.summary: Delinea has launched a new partner program called the Delinea Partner Advantage Program. The program offers identity security technology, protected discounts, and streamlined business processes for channel partners.

news.title: Microsoft’s July 2026 Patch Tuesday fixes 622 flaws and 2 exploited zero-days
news.summary: Microsoft's July 2026 Patch Tuesday addresses 622 CVEs, including actively exploited vulnerabilities in Active Directory Federation Services (AD FS) and SharePoint. The AD FS flaw is of direct relevance to IAM professionals as it affects federated identity and authentication infrastructure, alongside a disclosed BitLocker bypass requiring urgent patching.

news.title: Oak Raises $60M in Seed Funding to Build the AI-Native Identity Operating System
news.summary: Oak, founded by Shai Morag and Tal Marom, has raised $60M in seed funding to build an AI-native identity operating system. The platform aims to replace legacy identity architecture by governing all identities, including human and AI agent identities, for enterprise environments.

news.title: SailPoint、パートナーのイノベーションを促進する「ユニファイド プラットフォーム アクセス」プログラムを発表
news.summary: SailPoint announced a new program called "Unified Platform Access" aimed at enabling partner innovation, as announced on June 16, 2026 in the US. The program provides partners with access to SailPoint's unified platform, with this release being the Japanese translated version of the original US press release.

news.title: RabbitMQ Flaws Could Leak OAuth Secrets and Expose Cross-Tenant Queue Metadata
news.summary: Researchers at Miggo disclosed two access control vulnerabilities in RabbitMQ that can leak OAuth client secrets and allow cross-tenant queue metadata exposure. The flaws relate to improper access control in RabbitMQ's OAuth implementation, potentially enabling attackers to bypass tenant boundaries and compromise messaging infrastructure.

news.title: Configure Microsoft Entra Backup and Recovery
news.summary: Microsoft Entra Backup and Recovery reached general availability on June 30, 2026, as a built-in solution for Microsoft Entra ID. The feature automatically backs up critical directory objects daily, enabling restoration to a previous state after accidental changes or deletions.

news.title: New phishing kits target Microsoft 365 accounts, evade MFA
news.summary: Two new phishing kits named Jalisco and OmegaLord have been discovered targeting Microsoft 365 accounts using techniques that bypass multi-factor authentication. The kits represent active threats to MFA-protected environments, directly undermining a core IAM security control.

news.title: Microsoft Entra ID to enforce passkeys and retire legacy MFA by 2027
news.summary: Microsoft Entra ID will make passkeys the default authentication method for all public cloud tenants starting September 1, 2026, with full retirement of legacy SMS and voice-based MFA methods by 2027. Users currently relying on those legacy MFA methods will be automatically transitioned as part of the enforcement rollout.

news.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
news.summary: CISA released a postmortem report on a data breach in which a contractor accidentally exposed dozens of internal credentials, including AWS GovCloud keys, in a public GitHub repository for nearly six months. The incident highlights credential management failures and gaps in secrets detection practices relevant to IAM and privileged access management teams.

news.title: AI Agents Are a New Kind of Identity — and Most Organizations Aren't Ready
news.summary: The article addresses the emerging challenge of managing AI agents as a distinct identity type within IAM frameworks, arguing that existing approaches like service accounts and API tokens are insufficient. The piece calls for a fundamentally different identity management strategy to handle AI agents' unique authentication and authorization requirements.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the cybercrime group Scattered Spider pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to an August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and MFA bypass techniques used in high-profile breaches.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to bypass account security and reset passwords, successfully defacing high-profile Instagram accounts including the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot to perform unauthorized password resets were distributed via Telegram, enabling the account takeovers.
